They bought something didn't work out You can't control any of that stuff, right? So no matter what you do, okay People are going to enter with preconceived notions That's your starting point And it represents the starting point not just for your product It represents a starting point for where they feel about you the second 10 And the company that stands behind partner or three The preconceived notions are always going to be there But then the moment they enter the actual encounter itself And they lock eyes or they see you in person or hear your voice over the phone Everything changes All those beliefs they have based on those preconceived notions all those prejudgements They're either magnified dramatically Or reduced dramatically Or they remain the same Based on the actions that you take before you enter the encounter when it comes to bi-language This is stuff you can handle before you walk in the door One of the most common Things that I hear and it's really weird because I really go through this you know in certification I mean we break it down like you know, you know article of clothing by article of clothing, right? We have these healthy boundaries above and below the line Right so imagine Simultaneous like as you move so in open clothes, right? So imagine this here is your rapport meter, right? So in those first four seconds Boom a judgment gets made. Let's say you're at a you're at an eight on the rapport scale eight being pretty good rapport 10 being the highest and you start moving them down to straight line. You're at eight. Does it stay here? No It dips up and down constantly based on the last thing you say Based on the last tonality you used based on the last body language move or application that hit this person And they're rapport their sense of who you are. Do they feel that you care about them? Or are you just there to make a commission so watch imagine on one side of the equation a 10 Would represent this person really really cares about me They have my best interest at heart All they want to do is they want to help me solve my problems. That's a 10 rapport, right? A one rapport be this person couldn't care less about me All they want to do is make a commission whether the product is going to help me or not I can't trust this person as far as I can throw them. They don't care about me even one bit. That's a one Now in most cases, you're not going to really ever be so much at one or the other You're in the middle somewhere, right? Well, the idea is to stay close to a 10 as possible on that rapport scale But every time you speak They're judging you they're listening for cues both consciously and unconsciously and based on those conscious and unconscious cues They're going to go up and down They're not going to stay constant. They're just not but I will tell you this I will tell you this if you start off down here If in those first four seconds You do something something about your body language the way you look the way you dress the way you shake hands the way you fail to make eye contact You don't come back from that You really don't You can play some catch up walking after like, you know Four to ten seconds if you screw up the first four seconds and you're really poured on yeah, you got a shot But I'm telling you after that ten second mark You are judged You have been Mentalized you've been put in a box That who they think you are and you're going to spend the rest of the relationship until they buy Or not trying to fight out of a negative box Or trying to reinforce the good in the good box positive box you try to keep building on that That's it So what did I start off saying? You're crazy, right? But when we get there, okay? I go through exactly how this word by word, but let me just give you the basics here. Remember this, you know Body language is an instrumental aspect of pacing and leading How you enter someone's world where they are and you pace them And you pace them And you lead them in the direction that you want no meaning That you can affect someone else's body language. You can actually affect their their the way they feel about their their level of certainty on an emotional level by using pacing and leading so in other words when I meet somebody Okay, I'm gonna basically adopt I enter their world where they are I want to and by the end part of this just so you know it differs Based on if you could be in a different part of the world You could be in the deep south you could be in in the new york city very different how people act their mannerisms But the whole idea with pacing and leading Is you enter a prospects world where they are in other words The biggest miss the biggest lie Is opposites attract they don't opposites don't attract we attract people Based on our similarities things that we have in common not the other way around You dramatically tip the odds in your favor of getting into rapport When you enter a prospects world where they are which is exactly why things like excessive jewelry excessive amounts of perfume overly long hair or you know wild hairdos Clothing that's striking that doesn't seem like it's normal. That's what it's really it's all about that At the highest level what it really chunks up to is it stops It impedes your ability to pace and lead Because you're entering someone's world where they're not And sorry not what they expected you to enter it was essentially it cuts off your ability to Move someone from one state to another from one body language moves to another They get because you you have to it's always about entering someone's world where they are just remember that Whether you're in person or over the phone I want you to you know in in the body language module is all these exercises like we have crazy with the exercises Why it's an active let it's an active module. You have to actually you're you're reading you're learning you're doing you're changing You're it's happening on this very workshop style, right? Please Don't just say don't just skiggle. Oh, yeah, I know by please take this one to heart If you follow all the rules of body language I'm not saying you're gonna close people because of it What I'm saying is you set yourself up to have such an easy time of it Once you're perceived the right way Once people you know once they get that initial that gut reaction This is someone I want to do business with someone. That's an expert.
